<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);

    target->changed();

    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.registerThread();
    if (enableSafety) {
        thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    return thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    // Create reactive variables wrapping the input values
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr(b);

    });

    return calc->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // 3. Create batch ID
    auto batchId = graph.createBatch("test_batch");
    if (!batchId.has_value()) {
        return false; // Batch creation failed
    }

    // 4. Register nodes to batch
    graph.addObserverToActiveBatch(node1, *batchId);
    graph.addObserverToActiveBatch(node2, *batchId);

    // 5. Verify nodes are in batch
    if (!graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || !graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // 6. Unregister batch
    batchId.value().close();

    // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    if (graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // 8. Return success
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

 *

bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(target);

    // Set up initial relationship
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Verify initial notification
    source->changedNoNotify(); // Prevent immediate downstream notifications
    source->notify();
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {

    }

    // Reset nodes for next test

    bool success = true;

        // Transactional update that would create a cycle

            graph.addObserverInternal(target, source); // Hypothetical internal method
        });
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        success = false;
    }

    // Verify original relationship is preserved
    source->changedNoNotify();
    source->notify();
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return success;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addNode(target);

    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto obs = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(obs);
        graph->addNode(obs);
        obs->reset();  // Reset call count and changed state
    }

    for (auto& obs : observers) {
        graph->addObserver(target, obs);
    }

    target->notify();

    int total = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& obs : observers) {
        total += obs->getCallCount();
        if (obs->getCallCount() != 1 || !obs->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    return std::make_pair(total, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addNode(target);

    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto obs = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(obs);
        graph->addNode(obs);
        obs->reset();
    }

    for (auto& obs : observers) {
        graph->addObserver(target, obs);
    }

    target->notify();

    int total = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& obs : observers) {
        total += obs->getCallCount();
        if (obs->getCallCount() != 1 || !obs->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    return std::make_pair(total, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Set up chain relationships
    graph->addObserver(target, observer1);
    graph->addObserver(observer1, observer2);

    // Notify target
    target->notify();

    // Verify both observers were notified
    int total = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = (observer2->getCallCount() >= 1);

    // Return total call count and chain status
    return {total, chainStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_expression(int a, int b) {
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr::create(b);
    auto sum = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::add(varA, varB);
    auto expr = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::mul(sum, reaction::ValueExpr{2});
    return expr->evaluate().get<int>();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> reactiveVar(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to capture side effects
    std::atomic<int> capturedValue{initialValue};
    
    // 3. Create reactive action
    reaction::Action action([this](const reaction::Var<int>& var) {
        // Capture by reference to update the external variable
        capturedValue.store(var.get());
    }, reactiveVar);
    
    // 4. Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.set(newValue);
    
    // 5. Return captured value
    return capturedValue.load();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    auto firstCalc = reaction::createCalcExpr([=]() { return varA->get() + varB->get(); }, node);
    auto secondCalc = reaction::createCalcExpr([=]() { return firstCalc->get() * 2; }, node);
    varA->set(newA);
    return secondCalc->get();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables from input parameters
    auto var_a = reaction::Var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::Var(b);
    auto var_c = reaction::Var(c);

    // Create initial calculation: a + b
    auto calc = reaction::Calc([=]() {
        return var_a.get() + var_b.get();
    }, var_a, var_b);

    // Reset calculation to use new dependencies and expression: b * c
    calc.reset([=]() {
        return var_b.get() * var_c.get();
    }, var_b, var_c);

    // Return the result of the reset calculation
    return calc.get();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.getPtr()->get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.getPtr()->get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    auto calc = std::make_shared<reaction::Calc<std::string>>([=](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    }, person.m_name, person.m_age);

    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);

    return calc->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                           int initialB,
                                           int nonBatchA,
                                           int nonBatchB,
                                           int batchA,
                                           int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::Var{initialA};
    auto b = reaction::Var{initialB};
    
    // Track recomputation count
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::Var{0};
    
    // Create calculation that depends on a and b
    auto sumCalc = reaction::Calc<int>([=]() {
        return a.get() + b.get();
    }).observe(a).observe(b);
    
    // Observer to increment recompute count when sum changes
    reaction::Calc<void>([=]() {
        recomputeCount.set(recomputeCount.get() + 1);
    }).observe(sumCalc);
    
    // Non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int countAfterNonBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Batch updates
    {
        reaction::Batch batch;
        a.set(batchA);
        b.set(batchB);
    }
    int countAfterBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    
    return std::make_tuple(sumCalc.get(), countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch);
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create named reactive variables
    reaction::Var<int> varA("varA", valueA);
    reaction::Var<int> varB("varB", valueB);
    
    // Create a named calculation based on the variables
    reaction::Calc<int> calc("calcName", varA + varB);
    
    // Create a named action (no-argument, no-return)
    reaction::Action<void> action("actionName", []() {});
    
    // Collect names into a vector
    std::vector<std::string> names = {
        varA.getName(),
        varB.getName(),
        calc.getName(),
        action.getName()
    };
    
    // Combine names into a comma-separated string
    std::ostringstream oss;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < names.size(); ++i) {
        if (i > 0) {
            oss << ",";
        }
        oss << names[i];
    }
    
    return oss.str();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <tuple>

 *

 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL

```

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive nodes
    auto sensorVar = VarExpr{initialValue};
    auto filter = ChangeTrig{diffThreshold};
    auto filtered = CalcExpr{std::function<double(double)>([](double x) { return x; }), filter, sensorVar};

    auto statusNode = createNode(std::move(status));

    // Get recompute count after first update

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorNode->update(secondUpdate);

    // Get recompute count after second update
    int count2 = statusNode->getRecomputeCount();

    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusNode->getValue().get();

    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, count1, count2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive nodes
    auto sensorVar = VarExpr{initialValue};
    auto filter = ChangeTrig{diffThreshold};
    auto filtered = CalcExpr{
        std::function<double(double)>([](double x) { return x; }),
        filter,
        sensorVar
    };
    auto status = CalcExpr{
        std::function<std::string(double)>([levelThreshold](double x) {
            return x > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
        }),
        filtered
    };

    // Create nodes from expressions
    auto sensorNode = createNode(std::move(sensorVar));
    auto filteredNode = createNode(std::move(filtered));
    auto statusNode = createNode(std::move(status));

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensorNode->update(firstUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after first update
    int count1 = statusNode->getRecomputeCount();

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorNode->update(secondUpdate);

    // Record recompute count after second update
    int count2 = statusNode->getRecomputeCount();

    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusNode->getValue().get();

    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, count1, count2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    m_notificationCount++;
}

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount
inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields constructor
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::score and active accessors
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    ProfileFields profileFields(initialScore, false);
    FieldOwnerNode node;

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&node);

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindFieldsToOwner(profileFields, &node);

    profileFields.score() += scoreDelta;
    int notificationsAfterScore = node.getNotificationCount();

    profileFields.active() = !profileFields.active();
    int notificationsAfterStatus = node.getNotificationCount();

    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScore,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatus
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

// Implementation of RecordingNode::valueChanged to increment notification count
inline void RecordingNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) noexcept {

inline int RecordingNode::notificationCount() const noexcept {
    return m_notifications;

// Test function to verify observer state rollback functionality
inline RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes into the reactive graph
    graph.registerNode(node1);
    graph.registerNode(node2);

    // Establish initial observer dependency: node1 observes node2
    node1->addObCb(node2);

    // Save the current observer state for rollback
    auto savedState = graph.saveState();

    // Transactional update: Remove node2's observer (node1) to simulate dependency change
    {
        auto transaction = graph.beginTransaction();
        node2->removeObserver(node1);
        transaction.commit();
    }

    // Trigger value change on node1 (should NOT notify node2 due to removed observer)
    node1->valueChanged();

    // Capture notification count after dependency change
    int countAfterSecondBinding = node2->notificationCount();

    // Rollback to the original observer state
    graph.rollbackState(std::move(savedState));

    // Trigger value change on node1 again (should now notify node2)
    node1->valueChanged();

    // Capture notification count after rollback
    int countAfterRollback = node2->notificationCount();

    return RollbackObservation{
        .notificationsAfterSecondBinding = countAfterSecondBinding,
        .notificationsAfterRollback = countAfterRollback
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}

const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const noexcept { return m_name; }

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("A");
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("B");
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("C");
    
    graph.addNode(nodeA);
    graph.addNode(nodeB);
    graph.addNode(nodeC);
    
    graph.addObserver(nodeA, nodeB); // Direct relationship
    graph.addObserver(nodeB, nodeC); // Transitive relationship
    
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.collectObservers(nodeA, observers);
    
    bool hasDirect = !observers.find(nodeB).empty();
    bool hasTransitive = !observers.find(nodeC).empty();
    
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observers.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();
    threadManager.registerThread();

    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([this](const std::function<void()> &f = []() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread();
    }) {
        std::invoke(f);
    });

    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfter = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBefore = threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfter = threadCountAfter
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive nodes
    auto baseVar = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(baseValue);
    auto recomputeCount = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(0);
    auto deltaAVar = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(0);
    auto deltaBVar = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(0);

    // Create calculation expression
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<CalcExpr>([=]() {
        return baseVar->get() + deltaAVar->get() + deltaBVar->get();
    });

    // Add observer to increment recompute count on value change
    auto observer = ObserverFun([this](const NodePtr& node, const void* oldValue, const void* newValue) {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        if (oldValue != newValue) {
            recomputeCount->set(recomputeCount->get() + 1);
        }
    });
    calcExpr->addObCb(observer);

    // Record recompute count before batch
    int recomputeBefore = recomputeCount->get();

    // Execute batch updates
    Batch batch([](const NodePtr& node, const void* oldValue, const void* newValue) {});
    {
        BatchExeGuard g(batch);
        deltaAVar->set(deltaA);
        deltaBVar->set(deltaB);
    }

    // Record recompute count after batch
    int recomputeAfter = recomputeCount->get();

    // Get final value
    int finalValue = calcExpr->get();

    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on var and increments recomputeCount on change
    auto calc = reaction::Calc(
        [this](const int& v) { return v + 1; }, // Dummy operation
        [this](const int& oldVal, const int& newVal) {
            recomputeCount++;
        }
    );
    
    // Ensure the calculation observes the variable
    var.addObserver(calc);
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    // Add an action to update the variable (triggers the calculation)
    batch.add([this](const int& newValue) {
        var.set(newValue);
    });
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 10. Ensure calculation was evaluated (recomputeCount should be 1)
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        .closedInitially = closedInitially,
        .closedAfterClose = closedAfterClose,
        .recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

The code would look something like this:

namespace test_reaction {

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Create nodes
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    graph.registerNode(nodeD);
    
    // Set observer relationships
    nodeA->addObCb(nodeB);
    nodeB->addObCb(nodeC);
    nodeC->addObCb(nodeD);
    
    // Create NodeSet to collect observers
    NodeSet observersBefore;
    graph.getObservers(nodeA, observersBefore);
    
    // Populate cache by triggering valueChanged on B and C
    nodeB->changedNoNotify();
    nodeC->changedNoNotify();
    
    // Get stats before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    
    // Trigger cleanup
    graph.triggerCleanup();
    
    // Get stats after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = graph.getStats();
    
    // Close nodes
    graph.closeNode(nodeA);
    graph.closeNode(nodeB);
    graph.closeNode(nodeC);
    graph.closeNode(nodeD);
    
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = statsBefore.graphEntries,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = statsAfter.graphEntries,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = statsAfter.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

bool beforeEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
bool afterEnable = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
threadManager.reset();

return ThreadSafetyTransition{beforeEnable, afterEnable, afterReset, threadCount};

But wait, after registering the thread, the thread count should be 1. Also, before enabling, thread safety is disabled (false), after enabling it's true. After reset, it's back to false.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // 4. Get thread count
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 10. Return transition
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = before_enable,
        .afterEnable = after_enable,
        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a reactive graph context
    auto graph = std::make_shared<reaction::Graph>();
    
    // Create mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(baseValue);
    graph->addNode(mutableVar);
    
    // Create constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = std::make_shared<reaction::ConstVar<int>>(constValue);
    graph->addNode(constVar);
    
    // Create a sum expression using the AddOpExpr operator
    auto sumExpr = reaction::AddOpExpr::create(mutableVar, constVar);
    
    // Create a reactive node for the sum calculation
    auto sumNode = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcOpExpr>(sumExpr);
    graph->addNode(sumNode);
    
    // Get initial sum value
    int initialSum = sumNode->getValue();
    
    // Update the mutable variable
    mutableVar->update(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get updated sum value
    int updatedSum = sumNode->getValue();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

} // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    VarExpr<int> var(initialValue);
    VarExpr<int> counter(0);

    CalcExpr<int> calc([=](const int& v) {
        counter++;
        return v * 2;
    }, AlwaysTrig());
    calc.setInput(var);

    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter.get();
    var.set(initialValue);
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter.get();
    int forcedValue = calc.get();
    
    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = recomputeCountAfterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g;
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_value(value) {
    }
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() { return m_value; }
private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_value;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);

    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    auto& val1 = first.valueField();
    auto& val2 = second.valueField();
    val1 = initialFirst;
    val2 = initialSecond;

    // Read IDs
    uint64_t firstIdFirst = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecond = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();

    return FieldIdComparison{
        .firstIdFirstRead = firstIdFirst,
        .firstIdSecondRead = firstIdSecond,
        .secondId = secondId
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create a reactive variable
    auto var = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create a calculation with LastHandle policy
    auto calc = createCalc([var = var](const NodePtr& node) {
        return var->get();
    }, LastHandle{});
    
    // Step 3: Get initial result
    int initialResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 4: Update the source variable
    var->set(updatedValue);
    
    // Step 5: Get updated result
    int updatedResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 6: Close the source node
    var->close();
    
    // Step 7: Get result after source close
    int closedResult = calc->get();
    
    // Step 8: Return snapshot
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = closedResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {

    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <cstddef>

struct StackMonitorReport {

    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();

            report.guardAllocated = true;

    }

    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Step 1: Create lambda function to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload p;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            p.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return p;
    };

    // Step 2: Create small SBOResource
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource{initialSmall};

    // Step 3: Create large SBOResource
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource{createLargePayload()};

    // Step 4: Update small resource
    bool updateChanged = smallResource.update(updatedSmall);

    // Step 5: Get storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    // Step 6: Compile status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateChanged,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.isUsingSBO,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage,
        .largeInitialized = true,  // Assuming successful creation
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.isUsingSBO
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    auto& thread_manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();

    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;

    // Step 4: Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    bool trySharedNoSafety = mutex.trySharedNoSafety();
    
    // Step 5: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedNoSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    // Step 6: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        
        // Step 7: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held without safety
        bool tryUniqueNoSafety = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety();
        
        // Step 8: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueNoSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    // Step 11: Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    bool trySharedWithSafety = false;
    try {
        trySharedWithSafety = mutex.tryShared();
    } catch (...) {
        // Safely ignore exceptions thrown when lock is not acquired
    }
    
    // Step 12: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    // Step 13: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard again
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        
        // Step 14: Attempt to acquire unique lock with safety
        bool tryUniqueWithSafety = false;
        try {
            tryUniqueWithSafety = mutex.tryUnique();
        } catch (...) {
            // Safely ignore exceptions thrown when lock is not acquired
        }
        
        // Step 15: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    // Step 17: Return results
    return SharedMutexResult{
        .trySharedWithoutSafety = trySharedNoSafety,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety(),
        .trySharedWithSafety = trySharedWithSafety,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = tryUniqueWithSafety
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo.h>
#include <test_reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;

    // Get SBO traits for each pod type
    auto tiny_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::getInstance();
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = tiny_traits.m_always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = tiny_traits.m_force_heap;

    auto medium_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::getInstance();
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = medium_traits.m_conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = medium_traits.m_force_heap;

    auto large_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<LargePod>::getInstance();
    summary.largeForceHeap = large_traits.m_force_heap;

    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    auto config = reaction::SBOTraitsBase::getInstance();
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.m_sbo_always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.m_sbo_conditional_threshold;
    summary.maxStackUsage = config.m_sbo_max_stack_usage;

    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    auto var = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<int>>(*var * multiplier);
    auto calcReact = std::make_shared<reaction::React<int>>(calcExpr);

    int beforeResetValue = calcReact->getValue();
    threadManager.enableSafety();
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isSafetyEnabled();

    int newMultiplier = multiplier + 1;
    auto newCalcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<int>>(*var * newMultiplier);
    calcReact->reset(newCalcExpr);

    var->setValue(initialValue + 1);
    int afterResetValue = calcReact->getValue();

    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    for (const auto &code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::getErrorDescription(code));
    }
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,

        .originalMessage = ex.getOriginalMessage(),
        .file = ex.getFile(),
        .line = ex.getLine(),

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    auto resource = std::make_shared<Reaction::core::Resource<int>>(initialValue);
    auto ptr1 = resource->getPtr().get();
    int initial = *ptr1;
    
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    
    auto ptr2 = resource->getPtr().get();
    int after = *ptr2;
    
    bool stable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initial,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = after,
        .pointerStable = stable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Counter {
    reaction::VarExpr<int> value;
    void add(int delta) {
        value = value + delta;
    }
};

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Step 1: Define Counter struct (done inline above)
    
    // Step 2: Create reactive variable with Counter
    reaction::VarExpr<Counter> counterVar(Counter{initialValue});
    
    // Step 3: Get first pointer
    auto ptr1 = counterVar.getPtr();
    
    // Step 4: Call add method through pointer
    ptr1->add(delta);
    
    // Step 5: Get current value after first add
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = counterVar.get().value.get();
    
    // Step 6: Get second pointer
    auto ptr2 = counterVar.getPtr();
    
    // Step 7: Call add method through pointer again
    ptr2->add(delta);
    
    // Step 8: Get current value after second add
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = counterVar.get().value.get();
    
    // Step 9: Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    
    // Step 10: Return pointer access snapshot
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = valueAfterFirstAdd,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = valueAfterSecondAdd,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;

    // Create reactive variable
    auto var = std::make_unique<ReactVar<int>>(initialValue);

    // Create copy of source
    int copyVal = var->get();

    // Check validity before close
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = var->is_valid();

    // Check equality before close
    snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (var->get() == copyVal);

    // Close source node
    var->close();

    // Check validity after close
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = var->is_valid();

    // Check equality after close with exception handling
    try {
        int val = var->get();
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (val == copyVal);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var1 = std::make_shared<reaction::Var>(firstName);
    auto var2 = std::make_shared<reaction::Var>(secondName);
    auto var3 = std::make_shared<reaction::Var>(thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them
    std::set<reaction::ReactPtr> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);

    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react->getName());
    }

    // Check uniqueness of names
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == orderedNames.size());

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

ResetAwareNode::ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)), m_notifications(0) {}

int ResetAwareNode::notifications() const {
    return m_notifications.load();
}

void ResetAwareNode::clear() {
    m_notifications.store(0);
}

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addNode(source);
    graph->addNode(target);

    source->addObCb(target);

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedBeforeReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    target->clear();
    target->reset();

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedAfterReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    source->close();
    target->close();

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBeforeReset, notifiedAfterReset};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 3. Check small allocation without monitoring
    snapshot.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(1024);

    // 4. Enable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();

    // 5. Reinitialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // 7. Create StackGuard in scope
    {
        try {
            // Assuming StackGuard requires a size and a no-argument function to invoke on block
            StackGuard g(largeSize, []() {});
            snapshot.guardSucceeded = true;
        } catch (...) {
            snapshot.guardSucceeded = false;
        }

        // 9. Check if allocation blocked at limit
        snapshot.allocationBlockedAtLimit = !StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    }

    // 11. Check runtime allocation after release
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // 3. Check allocation without monitoring (already done)
    // 8. Check if guard succeeded (handled in try-catch)
    // 10. Exit guard scope (automatically handled)

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { 
        m_notifications++; 
        notify(changed); 
    }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
    mutable int m_notifications{0};
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create nodes
    reaction::ObserverNode owner("Owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("Field");

    // Step 2: Get graphs
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(&field);

    // Step 4: Register field under object ID
    fieldGraph.registerField(objectId, &field);

    // Step 5: Bind field to owner
    observerGraph.bind(&field, &owner);

    // Step 6: Trigger notification
    field.valueChanged();

    // Step 7: Check if owner was notified
    bool notifiedBefore = static_cast<const TrackingFieldNode*>(owner).notifications() == 1;

    // Step 8: Clear owner's notifications
    owner.clear();

    // Step 9: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearDependencies(&owner);

    // Step 10: Remove field from object ID mapping
    fieldGraph.removeObjFieldMap(objectId);

    // Step 11: Attempt to bind field again (no effect due to cleanup)
    observerGraph.bind(&field, &owner);

    // Step 12: Trigger notification again
    field.valueChanged();

    // Step 13: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = static_cast<const TrackingFieldNode*>(owner).notifications() == 0;

    // Step 14: Close nodes
    owner.close();
    field.close();

    // Step 15: Return result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    StackLimitSnapshot snapshot;
    auto& sm = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();

    // 1. Enable monitoring
    sm.enable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    sm.init();

    // 3. Get usage statistics before guard
    snapshot.statsBeforeGuard = sm.getUsageStats();

    // 4. Check if template allocation is allowed
    snapshot.templateAllocationAllowed = sm.isTemplateAllocationAllowed();

    // 5. Check if runtime allocation is allowed
    snapshot.runtimeAllocationAllowed = sm.isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();

    // 6. Create large StackGuard instance
    try {
        // Assuming StackGuard's constructor throws if it fails to allocate
        reaction::memory::StackGuard g(std::max(sm.getLimit() - sm.getUsage(), 0));
        snapshot.guardHeld = true;
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        snapshot.guardHeld = false;
    }

    // 7. Check if guard succeeded (handled in try-catch above)

    // 8. Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    snapshot.allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = !sm.isAllocationAllowed();

    // 9. Get usage statistics after guard
    snapshot.statsAfterGuard = sm.getUsageStats();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Void {};

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<Void>>();
    bool usesSboStorage = resource->isUsingSBO();
    auto val = resource->getValue();
    bool retrievedIsVoid = std::is_void_v<std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(val)>>;

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto nodePtr = resource->shared_from_this();
    observerGraph.addNode(nodePtr);

    nodePtr->setName(nodeName);
    bool nameAssigned = (nodePtr->getName() == nodeName);

    nodePtr->close();
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = (nodePtr->getName().empty());

    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(source1);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(source2);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(source3);

    source1->addObCb(observer);

    source1->notify();
    int first = observer->notifications();

    source1->removeObCb(observer);
    source2->addObCb(observer);
    source3->addObCb(observer);

    source1->notify();
    int firstAfter = observer->notifications();

    source2->notify();
    int second = observer->notifications();

    source3->notify();
    int third = observer->notifications();

    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();
    observer->close();

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfter,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = second,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = third
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    Var<int> var(0);
    Var<int> counter(0);
    Calc<int> calc([this](const VarExpr& expr) {
        return expr.get();
    }, [&](const Change<int>& change) {
        counter++;
    });

    int afterAdd = atomicAddAssign(var, 5);
    int afterMult = atomicMultiplyAssign(var, 3);
    int afterAnd = atomicBitwiseAndAssign(var, 0xF);
    int afterShift = atomicLeftShiftAssign(var, 2);
    int postRet = postIncrement(var);
    int finalVal = var.get();

    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAdd,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMult,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postRet,
        .finalValue = finalVal,
        .calcEvaluations = counter.get()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthProbeNode : ObserverNode {
    std::atomic<int> depth;
    DepthProbeNode() : depth(0) {}
};

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    auto& tm = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    shallow->depth.store(1);
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    deep->depth.store(2);

    NodeWeak s(shallow);
    NodeWeak d(deep);

    BatchCompare bc;

    bool initialResult = bc.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth.store(3);
    deep->depth.store(4);
    bool shallowBeforeDeepResult = bc.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth.store(5);
    bool deepBeforeShallowResult = bc.compare(d, s);

    NodeWeak null(nullptr);
    bool nullComesFirstResult = bc.compare(null, s);

    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeepResult,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallowResult,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirstResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthProbeNode : ObserverNode {
    std::atomic<int> depth;
    DepthProbeNode() : depth(0) {}
};

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    auto& tm = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    shallow->depth.store(1);
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    deep->depth.store(2);

    NodeWeak s(shallow);
    NodeWeak d(deep);

    BatchCompare bc;

    bool initialResult = bc.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth.store(3);
    deep->depth.store(4);
    bool shallowBeforeDeepResult = bc.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth.store(5);
    bool deepBeforeShallowResult = bc.compare(d, s);

    NodeWeak null(nullptr);
    bool nullComesFirstResult = bc.compare(null, s);

    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeepResult,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallowResult,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirstResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    auto a_var = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(a);
    auto b_var = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(b);
    auto c_var = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(c);

    auto and_expr = a_var && b_var;
    auto or_expr = a_var || c_var;

    bool initialAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool initialOr = or_expr.get();

    a_var.set(true);
    b_var.set(false);
    c_var.set(true);

    bool updatedAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool updatedOr = or_expr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    auto& cache = NodeMetricsCache::getInstance();
    auto node = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>("TestNode");
    
    const auto& statsBefore = cache.getStats();
    const auto& metricsBefore = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);
    
    cache.cacheNodeMetrics(node);
    
    const auto& metricsAfter = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);
    const auto& statsAfterCache = cache.getStats();
    
    cache.invalidateAll();
    
    const auto& metricsAfterInvalid = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);
    const auto& statsAfterInvalidate = cache.getStats();
    
    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries,
        .entriesAfterCache = statsAfterCache.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidate.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterCache.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterCache.missCount,
        .hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfterCache.hitCount) / (statsAfterCache.hitCount + statsAfterCache.missCount),
        .metricsCachedExists = (metricsAfter != nullptr),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = (metricsAfterInvalid == nullptr)
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    class IntCache : public CacheBase<int, int> {
    public:
        using CacheBase<int, int>::CacheBase;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::insert;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::lookup;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::getStats;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::invalidateAll;
        using CacheBase<int, int>::triggerCleanup;
    };

    auto cache = std::make_unique<IntCache>();
    auto statsBefore = cache->getStats();
    
    // Initial lookup (miss)
    cache->lookup(42);
    
    // Insert value
    cache->insert(42, 100);
    auto statsAfterInsert = cache->getStats();
    
    // Second lookup (hit)
    cache->lookup(42);
    
    // Invalidate and cleanup
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    auto statsAfterInvalid = cache->getStats();
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries,
        .entriesAfterInsert = statsAfterInsert.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalid.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInsert.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInsert.missCount,
        .hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfterInsert.hitCount) / 
                    (statsAfterInsert.hitCount + statsAfterInsert.missCount),
        .firstLookupWasMiss = (statsAfterInsert.missCount == 1),
        .secondLookupWasHit = (statsAfterInsert.hitCount == 1)};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
9. Return force register snapshot

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{
        .safetyBeforeForce = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfterMainForce = safetyAfterMain,
        .safetyAfterWorkerForce = safetyAfterWorker};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_types.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;
    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = std::ref(a);
    auto varB = std::ref(b);
    auto varC = std::ref(c);
    VarExpr<int> A(varA);
    VarExpr<int> B(varB);
    VarExpr<int> C(varC);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(A, B, OpAdd);
    NodePtr sumNode = expr(sumExpr);

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, OpNeg);
    NodePtr negNode = expr(negExpr);

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chainExpr = make_binary_expr(negExpr, C, OpMul);
    NodePtr chainNode = expr(chainExpr);

    // Get initial values
    RawExpressionSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.initialSum = sumNode->get();
    snapshot.initialNegatedSum = negNode->get();
    snapshot.initialChainedValue = chainNode->get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a += 1;
    c += 1;
    A.set(a);
    C.set(c);

    // Get updated values
    snapshot.updatedSum = sumNode->get();
    snapshot.updatedNegatedSum = negNode->get();
    snapshot.updatedChainedValue = chainNode->get();

    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the IntAtomicImpl class with atomic operation tracking.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        m_value = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = oldValue != m_value;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        // This implementation ignores forceNotify as per test requirements.
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        m_value = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = oldValue != m_value;
    }

    int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Implementation of the test function using reaction framework's atomic helpers.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl instance(initial);

    // Apply each operation and capture the change flags
    bool addChanged = reaction::atomicAddAssign(instance, 1);
    bool multiplyChanged = reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(instance, 2);
    bool andChanged = reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(instance, 0x3);
    bool leftShiftChanged = reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(instance, 1);
    bool incrementChanged = reaction::atomicIncrement(instance);

    // Get final value
    int32_t finalValue = instance.value();

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
